title: Plant RNA Purification Using TRIzol (TRI Reagent)
author: Diep R R Ganguly

steps.title: Collect 50-100 mg of plant tissue...
steps.instruction: Collect 50-100 mg of plant tissue and freeze immediately in liquid N 2 .

steps.title: Invert each tube by hand ~20x...
steps.instruction: Invert each tube by hand ~20x and incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes (DO NOT vortex samples as it may result in RNA degradation).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rcf for 10 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rcf for 10 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Repeat steps 4 and 5 (approx
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 4 and 5 (approx. 300-400 µL). Note, if you are observing buffer and salt carryover in your purified RNA (high 230 nm absorbance), reduce volume of upper-phase recovered.

steps.title: Add equal volume of 100% isopropanol,...
steps.instruction: Add equal volume of 100% isopropanol, then mix by inverting tubes ~20x by hand.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at 14,000 rcf for 20 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at 14,000 rcf for 20 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of 80 %...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of 80 % ethanol and invert tube ~10x.

steps.title: Remove supernatant, carefully since the pellet...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant, carefully since the pellet often becomes dislodged at this step.

steps.title: Air-dry pellet at room temperature for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Air-dry pellet at room temperature for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Make up volume of RNA solution to 87
steps.instruction: Make up volume of RNA solution to 87.5 μL with nuclease-free water. This can be performed with an aliquot or total sample from the previous step.

steps.title: Take 50-100 ng aliquot of RNA...
steps.instruction: Take 50-100 ng aliquot of RNA and mix 1:1 with 2X RNA loading dye (NEB).

steps.title: Grind tissue to fine powder under...
steps.instruction: Grind tissue to fine powder under liquid N 2 using tissue lyser or mortar + pestle, then immediately add 1 mL TRI reagent (1 mL per 100 mg tissue). Note, achieving a fine grind is critical to high yields of intact RNA.

steps.title: Transfer the upper aqueous phase to...
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper aqueous phase to a new microfuge tube (approx. 400-600 µL).

steps.title: Incubate at -20°C for 1 hour
steps.instruction: Incubate at -20°C for 1 hour. Alternatively, incubate overnight to capture small RNAs.

steps.title: Add 1/5 volume of pre-mixed chloroform:isoamyl...
steps.instruction: Add 1/5 volume of pre-mixed chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1), cap tubes, shake vigorously (by hand) for 15 seconds (solution should become cloudy), then incubate at room temperature for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant, you should observe a white pellet
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant, you should observe a white pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at 10,000 rcf for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at 10,000 rcf for 5 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in RNase-free water (e
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in RNase-free water (e.g. 0.01% DEPC-treated water) or Tris-EDTA (10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 6.5, 0.1 mM EDTA).

steps.title: (Optional) If the expected RNA concentration...
steps.instruction: (Optional) If the expected RNA concentration is 10 µg/mL, add 1/10 volume of 3M NaOAc (pH 5.5) and/or 100 µg/mL GlycoBlue (or glycogen).

steps.title: Add 10 μL Buffer RDD and 2
steps.instruction: Add 10 μL Buffer RDD and 2.5 μL DNase I stock solution (Qiagen RNase Free DNase Set) and mix with gentle pipetting.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 5-10 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 5-10 minutes.

steps.title: Add 500 μL of 100% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL of 100% ethanol.

steps.title: (Optional) Add 3 μL glycogen or...
steps.instruction: (Optional) Add 3 μL glycogen or GlycoBlue, and 10 μL NaOAc (pH 5.5) to aid RNA precipitation.

steps.title: Mix by gentle inversion
steps.instruction: Mix by gentle inversion.

steps.title: Incubate samples for at least 1...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples for at least 1 hour at -20 °C (overnight, if purifying small RNAs).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rcf at 4 °C for 20 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rcf at 4 °C for 20 minutes.

steps.title: Remove supernatant and rinse pellet with...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and rinse pellet with 1 mL of 80% ethanol.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 rcf for 5 minutes at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 rcf for 5 minutes at 4 °C.

steps.title: Remove supernatant without disturbing pellet and air-dry for 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant without disturbing pellet and air-dry for 2 minutes.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in RNase-free water (e
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in RNase-free water (e.g. 0.01% DEPC-treated water) or Tris-EDTA (10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 6.5, 0.1 mM EDTA).

steps.title: Load and run samples on a 1% agarose TBE gel
steps.instruction: Load and run samples on a 1% agarose TBE gel.

steps.title: Incubate RNA at 65 °C for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate RNA at 65 °C for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Nanodrop RNA to check for purity and quantity
steps.instruction: Nanodrop RNA to check for purity and quantity.
